tasks.title: Understand responsible AI principles
knowledge: Recognize risks and limitations of generative AI in software development
Understand ethical usage of AI coding assistants
Identify potential harms from AI-generated code (security, bias, copyright)
Describe mitigation strategies for AI-generated code risks
Understand responsible AI frameworks applicable to developer tools
skills: Evaluate AI-generated code for ethical and safety implications
Apply responsible AI principles when using Copilot
Identify when AI suggestions may introduce risks or biases
Make informed decisions about when to accept or reject Copilot suggestions

tasks.title: Validate and operate AI tools responsibly
knowledge: Understand the necessity of validating AI-generated output before use
Recognize that Copilot can produce hallucinations and incorrect suggestions
Know the principles for operating Copilot in a responsible and safe manner
Understand developer accountability for accepted AI-generated code
skills: Review and test AI-generated code before committing
Apply critical evaluation to Copilot suggestions
Operate Copilot as an assistant, maintaining developer oversight and accountability
Establish validation workflows for AI-assisted development

tasks.title: Use Copilot in the IDE
knowledge: Know how to enable GitHub Copilot in VS Code, JetBrains IDEs, and other supported editors
Understand different trigger modes: inline suggestions, Copilot Chat, slash commands
Know how to use Copilot in Agent Mode for multi-step autonomous tasks
Understand how content exclusions are applied in the IDE context
skills: Enable and configure Copilot in supported IDEs
Use inline completions, ghost text, and next suggestion navigation
Interact with Copilot Chat using natural language and slash commands
Invoke Agent Mode for complex multi-file editing tasks

tasks.title: Use GitHub Copilot CLI
knowledge: Define what GitHub Copilot CLI is and its purpose for terminal productivity
Know how to install the CLI: `gh extension install github/gh-copilot`
Understand key CLI features: `gh copilot explain`, `gh copilot suggest`
Know how to use CLI sessions for multi-turn terminal assistance
skills: Install and configure the GitHub Copilot CLI extension
Use `gh copilot explain` to understand unfamiliar shell commands
Use `gh copilot suggest` to generate commands from natural language
Generate scripts and command sequences using CLI assistance

tasks.title: Use Copilot features and capabilities
knowledge: Understand Agent Mode capabilities and when to use it
Know Copilot Edits for multi-file simultaneous editing
Understand Model Context Protocol (MCP) integration with Copilot
Know Copilot Sub-Agents, Extensions, and GitHub Copilot Spaces
Understand PR Summary generation and automatic code review
Know `.github/copilot-instructions.md` for repository-level customization
Understand GitHub Copilot Spark for web app creation
skills: Use Agent Mode for autonomous multi-step development tasks
Apply Copilot Edits to refactor across multiple files
Configure and use MCP servers with Copilot
Set up copilot-instructions.md for team-wide context customization
Generate PR summaries and request Copilot code reviews

tasks.title: Manage organization-wide settings and policies
knowledge: Know how to manage Copilot policies across an organization in GitHub admin settings
Understand Code Review policy configuration for Copilot-automated reviews
Know IDE availability settings (VS Code, JetBrains, etc.) and github.com availability
Understand audit log events available for Copilot governance
Know how to use the REST API for managing Copilot seat subscriptions
skills: Configure organization-level Copilot policies in GitHub admin settings
Enable and configure Copilot Code Review for pull requests
Assign and remove Copilot seats using the REST API or GitHub UI
Monitor Copilot usage via audit logs and admin dashboards

domains.title: Understand GitHub Copilot Data and Architecture

tasks.title: Describe data handling and flow
knowledge: Understand how user code (prompts) flows from IDE to GitHub Copilot servers
Know how context is collected and assembled into the prompt sent to the LLM
Understand the role of the Copilot proxy in filtering and processing
Know data sharing policies: what data is transmitted, retained, and used
Understand how suggestions are post-processed before being returned to the IDE
skills: Explain the end-to-end data flow from IDE input to suggestion display
Describe how prompts are constructed from editor context
Identify where data is filtered and processed in the Copilot pipeline
Apply knowledge of data handling to make informed privacy decisions

tasks.title: Understand code suggestion lifecycle and LLM limitations
knowledge: Understand the complete code suggestion lifecycle from trigger to display
Know fundamental LLM limitations: context window, hallucinations, training data cutoff
Understand the probabilistic nature of LLM outputs
Know why Copilot may produce outdated, incorrect, or insecure suggestions
skills: Describe the full lifecycle of a Copilot code suggestion
Apply knowledge of LLM limitations to evaluate suggestion quality
Identify when Copilot suggestions are likely to be outdated or incorrect
Use validation strategies to compensate for LLM limitations

domains.title: Apply Prompt Engineering and Context Crafting

tasks.title: Craft effective prompts
knowledge: Understand prompt structure and the elements of an effective Copilot prompt
Know how Copilot determines context from the active file, open tabs, and workspace
Understand zero-shot prompting: task without examples
Understand few-shot prompting: task with 2–5 concrete examples
Know prompt engineering best practices: specificity, descriptive naming, type annotations
skills: Write specific, context-rich prompts that produce accurate suggestions
Use descriptive variable and function names as natural prompt engineering
Apply few-shot examples to teach Copilot non-standard patterns
Structure comments to maximize Copilot suggestion quality

tasks.title: Engineer prompts for performance
knowledge: Understand prompt engineering principles: specificity, single responsibility, context richness
Know the Copilot prompt process flow: user input → context → model → response
Understand how chat history is used across a conversation session
Know when to use `#file:`, `@workspace`, and chat participants for context control
skills: Apply single-responsibility prompting for complex multi-step tasks
Use prompt process flow knowledge to engineer higher-quality prompts
Leverage chat history strategically within a Copilot Chat session
Reference specific files and workspace context using `#file:` and `@workspace`

domains.title: Improve Developer Productivity with GitHub Copilot

tasks.title: Enhance productivity and code quality
knowledge: Know how Copilot accelerates code generation for boilerplate and repetitive tasks
Understand how Copilot assists with refactoring and code modernization
Know how Copilot generates code documentation (docstrings, inline comments, README)
Understand how Copilot accelerates developer learning in unfamiliar codebases
Know how Copilot reduces context switching by providing answers within the IDE
Understand sample data and mock data generation use cases
Know legacy code modernization capabilities (Python 2→3, callbacks→async/await)
skills: Use Copilot to generate boilerplate, scaffolding, and repetitive code patterns
Apply Copilot Edits for refactoring and code modernization tasks
Generate docstrings, README sections, and inline documentation with Copilot
Use Copilot as an in-IDE learning tool for unfamiliar frameworks and APIs
Generate realistic test fixtures and mock data using Copilot

tasks.title: Support testing and security
knowledge: Understand how Copilot generates unit tests and integration tests
Know how Copilot identifies edge cases and boundary conditions
Understand how to generate test assertions with Copilot
Know how Copilot suggests security improvements in existing code
Understand performance optimization use cases with Copilot
skills: Generate unit and integration tests for existing functions using Copilot
Use Copilot to identify and test edge cases and boundary conditions
Write comprehensive test assertions with Copilot assistance
Use Copilot to identify and suggest security improvements
Apply Copilot for performance optimization in algorithms and queries

domains.title: Configure Privacy, Content Exclusions, and Safeguards

tasks.title: Manage privacy settings and content exclusions
knowledge: Know how to configure content exclusions at repository, organization, and IDE levels
Understand glob pattern syntax for content exclusion rules
Understand output ownership: who owns Copilot-generated code
Know the limitations around Copilot output IP and indemnification conditions
Understand individual user privacy controls vs. organizational policy controls
skills: Configure repository-level and organization-level content exclusions
Write correct glob patterns to exclude sensitive files and directories
Apply content exclusions for credentials, proprietary algorithms, and PII-containing files
Validate that content exclusions are working correctly after configuration

tasks.title: Apply safeguards and troubleshoot
knowledge: Know how to enable the suggestions matching public code (duplication detection) filter
Understand what the public code filter does and its IP indemnification implications
Know how to troubleshoot issues with Copilot suggestions not appearing
Understand how to troubleshoot content exclusions not taking effect
Know audit log events related to Copilot for governance oversight
skills: Enable and configure the public code filter in organizational settings
Troubleshoot missing suggestions by checking exclusion settings at all levels
Resolve content exclusion issues (reload IDE, verify glob patterns, check org policy priority)
Monitor Copilot usage and policy changes using GitHub audit logs
